title: lippia coarctata
intro: Lippia coarctata, also known as bushveld tea or wild bush tea, is a plant species in the verbena family, native to southern Africa. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Lippia coarctata include treating digestive issues, relieving fever, and soothing respiratory problems, as well as exhibiting antioxidant and anti-cancer properties. Lippia coarctata can be prepared as an herbal tea by infusing the leaves in hot water, or it can be used as a tincture or infused oil for various health remedies.
